channel.title: equations on Christos Tsanikidis - CS, Math, Life
channel.description: Recent content in equations on Christos Tsanikidis - CS, Math, Life

item.title: Counting the number of elements in Sequences made simple
item.description: Counting the number of elements in a sequence of elements can often be confusing, and this is something that frequently comes up in mathematics, computer science or even programming (I won’t get deeper in the use cases in this post). Thankfully there is a simple method that I am using in such scenarios which makes these mini problems straightforward. Only basic mathematics are required. Examples First Let’s start in an unorthodox fashion, providing a few examples before the description.

item.title: Hierarchies - Logarithmic counting for humans
item.description: Assumption: Rearrangement of objects for counting is possible. People are capable of processing and understanding a very specific range of patterns with ease. Likely a subset of those relevant to our past survivability. For instance we are unable to enumerate instantly small number of objects - for today’s requirements - and therefore we have to reduce the problem into maintanable chunks, appropriate for our capabilities. A way to reduce the problem is building hierarchies.
